# Odometer Tracking

> Track cumulative vehicle mileage either by reading the hardware odometer transmitted by the GPS device or by calculating distance from GPS coordinates, with per-position incremental distances and a...

**Category:** Data · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** gps · tracking · odometer · mileage · distance · fleet · maintenance

## What this does

Track cumulative vehicle mileage either by reading the hardware odometer transmitted by the GPS device or by calculating distance from GPS coordinates, with per-position incremental distances and a...

Specifies 4 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **odometer** *(number, optional)* — Hardware odometer reading in metres, transmitted directly by the vehicle ECU via the device
- **distance** *(number, optional)* — Incremental distance in metres calculated from the previous position to this one
- **total_distance** *(number, optional)* — Cumulative distance in metres accumulated across all positions for this device from the tracking ...
- **min_distance_meters** *(number, optional)* — Minimum position-to-position distance to record; movements shorter than this are treated as GPS n...
- **max_distance_meters** *(number, optional)* — Maximum plausible position-to-position distance; movements longer than this are treated as GPS er...

## What must be true

- **rule_1:** If the device transmits a hardware odometer value, it is stored directly on the position and takes precedence over calculated distance in reports
- **rule_2:** Incremental distance is calculated using the great-circle formula between the current and previous position coordinates
- **rule_3:** Distance increments shorter than min_distance_meters are treated as GPS noise and recorded as zero
- **rule_4:** Distance increments longer than max_distance_meters are treated as GPS position errors and excluded from the cumulative total
- **rule_5:** Cumulative total_distance is maintained on each position record as a running sum of valid increments
- **rule_6:** Reports prefer the hardware odometer if available; otherwise they use total_distance
- **rule_7:** The odometer can be reset remotely via a set_odometer command to align with a workshop reading

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Error Position Excluded** — when incremental distance > max_distance_meters, then Implausible jump excluded from odometer accumulation; position is still stored.
- **Noise Filtered** — when incremental distance < min_distance_meters, then Position recorded with zero distance increment; cumulative total unchanged.
- **Hardware Odometer Used** — when device transmits a non-zero hardware odometer value in position attributes, then Hardware odometer recorded; used by maintenance reminders and summary reports in preference to calculated distance.
- **Distance Calculated** — when a new position is received with valid coordinates; a previous position exists for the device; incremental distance >= min_distance_meters; incremental distance <= max_distance_meters, then Distance fields populated on the position record; available for reports and maintenance triggers.

## Errors it can return

- `ODOMETER_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND` — The device referenced does not exist

## Events

**`odometer.updated`** — A new distance increment was calculated and accumulated (emitted when crossing a reporting threshold)
  Payload: `device_id`, `total_distance`, `increment`, `position_id`

## Connects to

- **gps-position-ingestion** *(required)* — GPS coordinates and hardware odometer values arrive via position ingestion
- **maintenance-reminders** *(recommended)* — Maintenance intervals are triggered when odometer values cross configured thresholds
- **trip-detection** *(recommended)* — Trip distance is derived from odometer or calculated distance over the trip interval
